name: Bristow Group Inc.
business: Bristow Group Inc. is the leading global provider of innovative and sustainable vertical flight solutions. It primarily provides aviation services to offshore energy companies and government entities, including personnel transportation, search and rescue, medevac, fixed wing transportation, unmanned systems and ad-hoc helicopter services.

exposures.sentence: We primarily provide aviation services to a broad base of offshore energy companies and government entities.
Our government customers primarily outsource SAR activities whereby we operate specialized helicopters and provide highly trained personnel.
Bristow has deepened partnerships with multiple manufacturers to assist with the operationalization of AAM.

In February 2026, Bristow completed its first electric aviation test project, conducted as an international test arena alongside partners BETA Technologies, Inc. (Beta), Avinor and Norway CAA, using BETA's ALIA CX300 aircraft.

In January 2026, Bristow announced it had signed a deposit agreement with Electra.aero, Inc. (Electra) to secure the first delivery slots for five Electra EL9 Ultra Short hybrid-electric aircraft, subject to aircraft certification.

In December 2025, Bristow announced its expanded role in advancing the UK's first electric air taxi network through a new collaboration with Vertical Aerospace (Vertical) and Skyports Infrastructure (Skyports), with initial service targeted for early 2029.
Our aviation services include personnel transportation, search and rescue (SAR), medevac, fixed wing transportation, unmanned systems and ad-hoc helicopter services.

We encourage and assist our engine manufacturers, aircraft manufacturers, our customers and other stakeholders to be early and leading adopters of sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) as we encourage wider availability of these alternative fuels by our fuel suppliers.

The potential for cyberattacks or security breaches that could disrupt operations, compromise confidential or sensitive information, damage reputation, expose to legal liability, or cause financial losses

catalysts.event: Launch of quarterly cash dividend program
catalysts.sentence: On February 25, 2026, Bristow launched its quarterly cash dividend program and declared a dividend of $0.125 per share of its common stock.

catalysts.event: Closing of $500 million 6.750% Senior Secured Notes offering
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, Bristow closed a private offering of $500 million aggregate principal amount of 6.750% Senior Secured Notes due 2033

catalysts.event: Amendment and restatement of ABL Facility with extended maturity to 2031
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, Bristow entered into an amendment and restatement of its asset-based revolving credit facility (the ABL Facility), which, among other things, extended the maturity date to January 26, 2031

catalysts.event: Completion of first electric aviation test project in Norway
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, Bristow completed its first electric aviation test project, conducted as an international test arena alongside partners BETA Technologies, Inc. (Beta), Avinor and Norway CAA, using BETA's ALIA CX300 aircraft.

catalysts.event: Signed deposit agreement for five Electra EL9 hybrid-electric aircraft
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, Bristow announced it had signed a deposit agreement with Electra.aero, Inc. (Electra) to secure the first delivery slots for five Electra EL9 Ultra Short hybrid-electric aircraft, subject to aircraft certification.

catalysts.event: Expanded role in UK electric air taxi network with Vertical Aerospace and Skyports
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, Bristow announced its expanded role in advancing the UK's first electric air taxi network through a new collaboration with Vertical Aerospace (Vertical) and Skyports Infrastructure (Skyports), with initial service targeted for early 2029.

catalysts.event: Final Irish SAR base becomes fully operational at Waterford Airport
catalysts.sentence: On February 1, 2026, Bristow's last Irish SAR base went live at Waterford Airport, in south-east Ireland.
The transition to the previously announced UKSAR2G contract also commenced in the fourth quarter of 2024, and the last base will finish transitioning in late 2026.

catalysts.event: Delivery of seven AW189 heavy helicopters
catalysts.sentence: As of December 31, 2025, we had unfunded capital commitments of $104.4 million, consisting primarily of agreements to purchase seven AW189 heavy helicopters scheduled to be delivered in 2026
